formula_or_rule: Corrected sodium (mEq/L) = measured sodium (mEq/L) + 2.4 * ((serum glucose mg/dL - 100) / 100). This is the Hillier correction factor (2.4 mEq/L Na rise per 100 mg/dL glucose above normal). An older, less aggressive correction (Katz) uses a factor of 1.6 instead of 2.4 - if the expected answer doesn't match with 2.4, try 1.6.

condition_rules.no_correction_needed: only apply the correction if glucose > 100 mg/dL
